claim: The text-only section (7 folios, entropy 3.9016 bits, highest of all sections) encodes unenciphered or lightly enciphered text in a language with entropy naturally near 3.9 bits, consistent with medieval Italian vernacular (estimated entropy 3.85-3.95 bits) rather than Latin (estimated ~4.0 bits) or a heavily enciphered text.
evidence_for: Text-only section entropy 3.9016 bits is the highest section, closest to natural-language entropy
Italian vernacular entropy estimate (~3.85-3.95 bits) brackets the observed value more tightly than Latin (~4.0 bits)
Belief weight 0.624-0.677 already supports text-only section as closest to natural language
If this section is lightly enciphered, it is the best decipherment entry point
evidence_against: Small sample size (7 folios, 2,349 words) makes entropy estimates unreliable
High entropy could reflect a more complex cipher rather than less encipherment
Latin entropy estimate may overlap with Italian depending on corpus used for comparison
test: Compute per-section glyph entropy with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals; fit a character-level n-gram model trained on medieval Latin and medieval Italian to the text-only section glyphs under candidate simple-substitution mappings; measure cross-entropy and perplexity for Latin vs. Italian models across all sections, expecting text-only to have lowest perplexity under the best-fit natural language model.
test_metric: Bootstrap confidence intervals on per-section entropy; cross-entropy of text-only section under Latin and Italian n-gram models across top-1000 random substitution mappings
test_threshold: Confirmation: text-only section achieves cross-entropy below 5.5 bits/glyph under Italian n-gram model for at least one substitution mapping, outperforming Latin model by > 0.3 bits. Denial: neither Latin nor Italian model achieves cross-entropy below 6.0 bits for any tested substitution, consistent with a non-Romance or heavily enciphered source.

tests_run.test: compute per-section glyph entropy with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals; fit a character-level n-g
tests_run.details: Observed glyph entropy: 3.8627 bits. Expected for hypothesis: ~4.0 bits. Difference: 0.1373 bits.

reasoning: The text-only section is the highest-entropy, lowest-illustration section; if it encodes natural language with minimal transformation, it is the optimal crib source for testing specific language hypotheses against the full corpus.
